Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, further supporting possible renal dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, indicating no evidence of chronic hyperglycemia. No signs of urinary tract infection are present. Recommend further nephrological evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and assessment of underlying causes.
